reasoning:

For the second date (Jan 20, 2026), the Jan 1 list will be used. Wesley So is not reported to have played classic tournaments in December. However, other players active in Dec (like Abdusattorov, who won the London Chess Classic) could gain enough points to surpass his 2753 rating. Also, the zero-sum World Championship match between Gukesh (#10, 2754) and Ding (#16, 2734) means if Ding surpasses So, Gukesh will likely fall below So, creating a neutralizing effect. Given the close proximity of ratings, there is a moderate chance someone passes So, but he remains slightly favored to hold <= 11.
For the third date (March 21, 2026), the January (Tata Steel) and February tournaments will add substantial variance, bringing the probability closer to the base rate of ~0.5.
